expressions.notes:
이상하다 = to be weird, odd, strange
이상한 is the adjective form of 이상하다.

난처하다 = to be in an awkward situation
난처한 is the adjective form of 난처하다.

날카롭다 = to be sharp; to be penetrating
날카로운 is the adjective form of 날카롭다.

바보같다 = to be stupid, foolish
바보같은 is the adjective form of 바보같다.

개인적이다 = to be personal, private
개인적인 is the adjective form of 개인적이다.
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

하나 = one (native Korean number)
-이/이/예도 됼까요? = (polite) Can + S + V?

expressions.english: I think the question is a little bit strange.

expressions.notes:
먼저 = first; before + N
-이/이/여 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.english: It's such a difficult question to answer.
expressions.notes:
참 = very, quite
-(은)네요 is a sentence ending that expresses realization of a fact or agreement to a statement.

expressions.english: I don't want to answer that question.
Please don't ask any personal questions.
expressions.notes:
질문을 하다 = to ask a question
-지 말아 주세요 = Please don't + V.

expressions.english: If you have any questions, please raise your hand.
expressions.notes:
손 = hand
들다 = to raise; to pick (something) up

expressions.english: We will take questions after the presentation is finished.
expressions.notes:
발표 = presentation
끝나다 = to end; to be over
다음에 = next time; later
받다 = to receive

expressions.english: I will answer that question later in private.
expressions.notes:
나중에 = next time; later
-(으)ㄹ게요 = I promise I will + V; I will + V

expressions.korean: 모르는 게 있으면 바로바로 질문해 주세요.
expressions.english: If you have something that you don't know, please ask me immediately.
expressions.notes:
모르다 = to not know
바로바로 = immediately
